scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT COURT / CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE

3. The Plaintiff states that the present suit is subject to the provisions of Section 12A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The Plaintiff has {{SECTION_12A_COMPLIANCE_STATUS}} with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement prior to filing this plaint.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a written Agreement to Sell regarding Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement stipulated that the sale deed was to be executed on or before 10.07.2024. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

6. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654). The Plaintiff has duly performed this obligation under the contract. The bank receipt evidencing this payment is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

7. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has arranged the necessary funds to discharge this liability and has remained capable of paying the balance amount as per the agreed timeline.

8. Throughout the period from the execution of the Agreement until the date of filing this plaint, the Plaintiff has remained ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has maintained the availability of funds and has taken all necessary steps to ensure the completion of the transaction within the stipulated time.

9. To compel the Defendant to perform his obligation, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed as per the Agreement. The legal notice along with the postal receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

BREACH PARTICULARS

10. On 05.06.2024, prior to the date fixed for execution, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed. The Defendant cited the receipt of a higher offer from a third party as the reason for his refusal to honor the Agreement entered into with the Plaintiff. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

11. On the date fixed for execution, being 10.07.2024, the Defendant failed to appear for the registration of the sale deed and did not execute the document in favor of the Plaintiff. The Defendant remained in default of his contractual obligation to transfer the title of the suit property to the Plaintiff.

12. Despite the issuance of the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, the Defendant did not comply with the demand to execute the sale deed. The Defendant has persisted in his refusal to perform the contract, thereby committing a breach of the Agreement to Sell.

DAMAGES

13. Due to the Defendant's breach of the Agreement, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss and prejudice. The Plaintiff was deprived of the property as agreed, and the market value of the suit property has escalated since the date of the Agreement, causing loss to the Plaintiff. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the direct and natural consequences of the Defendant's breach.

14. The Plaintiff claims damages for the loss suffered due to the Defendant's refusal to execute the sale deed and the subsequent delay. The quantification of the damages is based on the market difference and incidental costs incurred by the Plaintiff. The Plaintiff claims damages amounting to Rs. {{DAMAGES_AMOUNT}}/- along with costs of the suit.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

16. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

18. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Order XX Rule 11 CPC and Section 34 CPC respectively;
    (e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

SCHEDULE OF SUIT PROPERTY

{{SCHEDULE_OF_SUIT_PROPERTY -- Address, Survey/Plot No., Extent/Area, Boundaries (East/West/North/South)}}

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024 between Plaintiff and Defendant

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654) for advance payment

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defe

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director: Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership,
Having its office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner: Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides and carries on business within the local limits of this Court.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. The Plaintiff confirms compliance with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. Pre-institution mediation was conducted through the Mediation and Conciliation Centre, Bengaluru, on {{MEDIATION_DATE}}. The mediation was unsuccessful and a certificate of non-settlement was issued on {{MEDIATION_CERTIFICATE_DATE}}. A copy of the mediation certificate is annexed hereto as Annexure F.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system. The total contract value was agreed at Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the Agreement is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT (UTR: ICIC20240201456789). A copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

8. Under the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024.

9. The Plaintiff has duly performed all obligations required on its part under the Agreement, including the payment of the agreed advance consideration.

BREACH PARTICULARS

10. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

11. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the development or returning the advance payment. Email correspondence evidencing the abandonment is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to perform its obligations. A copy of the legal notice is annexed hereto as Annexure E. The Defendant has failed to comply with the notice.

DAMAGES

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant, which constitutes the direct loss suffered due to the breach.

14. The Plaintiff further suffered consequential damages amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by the non-delivery of the ERP system. The loss assessment report by the auditor quantifying this loss is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

15. The total damages claimed by the Plaintiff amount to Rs. 32,00,000/-, being the direct and natural consequence of the Defendant's breach of contract.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the contractual deadline, thereby breaching the Agreement and causing the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

19. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to the ad valorem rate applicable to money suits and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at 18% per annum from the date of breach (01.05.2024) until the date of institution of the suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

    The interest is justified as compensation for loss naturally arising from the breach under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- as damages for breach of contract;
    (b) Award pre-suit interest at 18% per annum from 01.05.2024 till the date of institution of the suit;
    (c) Award pendente lite interest at the rate of 18% per annum from the date of institution of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (e) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Refund of advance payment
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis: First milestone payment made on 01.02.2024, not returned upon breach
     Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT receipt)

(ii) Consequential damages for operational delay
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis: Loss assessment report by auditor quantifying business loss due to non-delivery
     Documen
